issue_claim: rounding up one wei collateral during liquidation lets a borrower self-liquidate and create bad debt
why_it_looked_bad: liquidation math that rounds up can allocate an entire dust collateral amount for a tiny debt repayment, which resembles a bad-debt creation vector in minimal collateral states
why_not_valid: the public Silo/Cantina report treated this as Low/Informational because the scenario depended on economically unlikely 1 wei collateral representing meaningful value and no working PoC was produced
downgrade_reason: requires extreme token valuation/min-collateral configuration and lacks demonstrated profitable path; use it as a rounding test idea, not an HM finding by itself
trust_assumption: market parameters enforce sane minimum collateral or token decimal/value constraints
when_it_could_be_real: the protocol supports low-decimal/high-value collateral where 1 unit is economically meaningful
rounding direction combines with self-liquidation to create measurable bad debt
no minimum collateral/debt size prevents dust positions
